data.title: Bharat Dynamics Clocks Gains in Fiscal Q3 Profit
data.description:
Bharat Dynamics recorded a rise in its profit to 1.47 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 1.35 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share came in at 4.01 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 3.68 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the defense equipment manufacturer.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 also increased to 8.32 billion rupees from 6.02 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board declared an interim dividend of 4 rupees per share of the face value of 5 rupees each for the fiscal year ending March 31.

Shares of the company were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Trade War Between U.S. and China Could Weaken Eurozone Economy, Says ECB's Cipollone
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

A trade war between the U.S. and China could lead to weaker economic growth and falling inflation in the eurozone, a top official at the European Central Bank warned in an interview published Thursday.

Speaking to Reuters, ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone said high U.S. tariffs on imports from the world's second largest economy could push Chinese businesses to look for buyers elsewhere, and increase their competition with eurozone rivals.

"Trade barriers will force China to sell its goods elsewhere and the competition from China could be a serious threat to us," he said. "These goods showing up in Europe could have both a deflationary and a contractionary impact because they would crowd out local products."

The U.S. and China entered a new trade war on Tuesday, with tariffs of 10% going into effect on Chinese goods. Beijing struck back with 10%-15% levies that will take effect Feb. 10 on select American goods.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: A.P. Moller-Maersk Swings to Profit as Red Sea Disruptions Boost Freight Rates — Update
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

A.P. Moeller-Maersk swung to a better-than-expected net profit in the fourth quarter as disruptions to the Red Sea shipping supported higher shipping rates.

The Danish shipping company said it made $2.085 billion in net profit for the quarter, compared with a net loss of $436 million a year ago, when an oversupply of cargo capacity weighed on freight rates.

Quarterly revenue came to $14.59 billion, a 24% on-year increase as continued disruption to shipping lanes in the Red Sea pushed up shipping rates by 38% in the quarter.

The result compares with analysts' expectations of $1.31 billion in net profit on $13.89 billion in revenue, according to a Visible Alpha consensus.

Maersk's ocean segment, its container shipping business and the company's largest division, posted a 39% increase to revenue in the reporting period.

The company said it would propose a 1,120 Danish kroner dividend after separately announcing Wednesday evening the start of a share buyback program of up to around $2 billion.

The company said that for 2025 it is targeting $6 billion to $9 billion in underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, up to $3 billion in underlying earnings before interest and taxes.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Trade War Between U.S. And China Could Weaken Eurozone Economy, ECB's Cipollone Says — Update
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

A trade war between the U.S. and China could lead to weaker economic growth and falling inflation in the eurozone, a top official at the European Central Bank warned in an interview published Thursday.

Speaking to Reuters, ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone said high U.S. tariffs on imports from the world's second largest economy could push Chinese businesses to look for buyers elsewhere, and increase their competition with eurozone rivals.

"Trade barriers will force China to sell its goods elsewhere and the competition from China could be a serious threat to us," he said. "These goods showing up in Europe could have both a deflationary and a contractionary impact because they would crowd out local products."

The U.S. and China entered a new trade war on Tuesday, with tariffs of 10% going into effect on Chinese goods. Beijing struck back with 10%-15% levies that are set to take effect on Feb. 10 on select American goods.

The ECB last week lowered its key interest for a fifth time since June 2024, and investors expect further cuts in upcoming meetings of its governing council as inflation cools and economic growth remains weak.

However, Cipollone said that given the high levels of uncertainty about the economic outlook that partly derives from U.S. trade policy, "anything can happen."

"We all agree there is still room for adjusting rates downwards," he said. "But we need to be extremely careful."

U.S. President Trump has made it clear that U.S. allies across the Atlantic are high on his target list for tariffs. But Cipollone said the imbalances in trade between the two economic giants are smaller than they appear.

"It's true we have a goods surplus with the United States," he said. "But if you add in services and look at the overall current account, then the balance is close to zero."

ECB rate setters have become more concerned about the outlook for growth in the eurozone economy, which stagnated in the final quarter of last year.

Cipollone said that disappointing outcome might be a reflection of uncertainty about the outlook for the global economy in the run-up to the U.S. presidential election and its aftermath.

"The real problem is investments, and that is only partially linked to monetary policy," he said. "The culprit is uncertainty. Investments have been weak since the summer given the overall uncertainty and the direction of trade policy after the US election."

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: Anglo American Cuts 2025 Diamond View After Production Falls
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Anglo American reported a fall in fourth-quarter copper and diamond production and cut its 2025 diamond production guidance.

The London-listed mining giant said Thursday that it dug out 198,000 tons of copper, 14% less than in the fourth quarter of 2023, due to the planned shut down of its smaller Los Bronces plant.

Diamond production also fell 26% on year to 5.8 million carats from 7.9 million carats as Anglo American cut production in response to lower demand and higher than normal levels of inventory.

Anglo American also cuts its full-year 2025 diamond production guidance to between 20 million to 23 million carats, down from previous guidance of 30 million to 33 million carats.

The company kept its full-year guidance for copper production for 2025 and 2026 unchanged.

Across the business, all division met their full-year production guidance, the company said.

Anglo American also said it is making "excellent" progress with its portfolio simplification. The sales process for its nickel business is well progressed, and its continue to prepare the De Beers business for separation, the company said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Jingxi Culture & Tourism Logs 1.01 Billion Yuan in Box Office Revenues
data.description:
Beijing Jingxi Culture & Tourism generated 1.01 billion yuan in box office revenues after the release of the movie The Second Part of the Conferred God: War in Xiqi on Jan. 29, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Wednesday.

The figures exceeded 50% of the operating income of the company's audited consolidated financial statements for the most recent fiscal year.

The Chinese tour operator's operating income from the film is expected to be between 90 million yuan and 120 million yuan as of Tuesday, Feb. 4, the disclosure said.

The company's shares dropped over 6% at the close.

data.title: Dallah Health's Capital Increase Gets Saudi Regulatory Nod
data.description:
Dallah Healthcare (SASE:4004) said Thursday it secured clearance from Saudi's Capital Market Authority for its capital increase.

The regulatory approval is related to the healthcare services company's acquisition of a 97.41% stake in Al-Ahsa Medical Services and 100% of Al-Salam Medical Services from Ayyan Investment (SASE:2140).

Additionally, the companies agreed to extend the agreement by another two months to complete the deal.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0750 GMT - China's equity markets could have a tough year regardless of how the trade tensions play out, Capital Economics analyst Thomas Mathews says in a research note. Investors may be overly optimistic about the likelihood of a reprieve on tariffs, the analyst says, and tariffs are not the only reason to be downbeat. The prospects of further fiscal stimulus, which investors have put high hopes on, are uncertain, he says. "A separate problem is that Chinese companies have struggled for a long time to generate sustained growth in earnings per share," he adds. Therefore, there's play of room for conditions in China's markets to deteriorate if the tariffs remain in place, he adds.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher amid receding concerns of trade friction between the U.S. and its trading partners. Brokerage and electronics stocks led the gains. Nomura Holdings rose 3.8% after its nine-month net profit more than doubled. Renesas Electronics surged 13% after its 4Q results. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.6% to 39066.53. USD/JPY is at 152.64, compared with 152.60 as of Wednesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. Investors are closely watching for developments regarding U.S. tariffs. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2 basis points to 1.260%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0724 GMT - Chinese shares ended higher, with the Shanghai Composite Index rising 1.3% to 3270.66. The Shenzhen Composite Index added 2.3% to 1964.55 and the ChiNext Price Index gained 2.8% to 2120.75. Investors are focusing on developments regarding tariffs. Among major stocks, BYD shares rose 10% on high expectations for its upcoming smart car press conference. SMIC added 3.0% and Great Wall Motor gained 2.1%. Decliners included Beijing Kingsoft Office Software, which was 1.2% lower, and China Construction Bank, which was 0.5% lower. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 73 points higher, or 0.9%, according to IG. The index closed 52 points higher, or 0.6%, to 8523 points Wednesday. Market sentiment across Europe and the U.S. has improved on waning trade tensions with President Trump, Swissquote Bank analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. Investors are also looking ahead to the latest policy decision from the Bank of England at 1200 GMT. The BOE is expected to lower interest rates by 25 basis points as U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves's growth plans are "clouded by the country's restricted finances and the economy calls for BOE support," she says.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0719 GMT - Societe Generale's better-than-expected results and guidance for 2025 that points to more cost controls should provide confidence that its 2026 targets are in reach, RBC Capital Markets' Anke Reingen and Matthew Russell say in a research note. The French lender delivered a good set of results, the analysts say. An increase to its payout ratio to half of net profit from 2024 onward isn't totally surprising, RBC says. Moreover, Societe Generale is targeting a return on tangible equity--a key profitability metric--of more than 8% in 2025 and reiterated its guidance of a return of 9%-10% next year. This should give confidence on its outlook, given that consensus expectations are close to the low end of its 2025 guidance but remain below Societe Generale's 2026 targets, RBC says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi rose 1.1% to close at 2536.75, extending gains for a third consecutive session. Construction, internet and semiconductor stocks advanced. Wall Street's overnight gains helped buoy sentiment. Foreign and institutional investors were net buyers. Mobile internet company Kakao rose 4.9%, extending its rally after agreeing earlier this week with OpenAI to develop artificial-intelligence products. Daewoo Engineering & Construction gained 4.2% after its 4Q earnings beat. Memory-chip maker SK Hynix added 2.4%. Meanwhile, KB Financial Group fell 6.7% after its 4Q earnings miss. USD/KRW settled 0.2% higher at 1,447.70 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was down 4.2 bps at 2.776%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0549 GMT - Global Power Synergy's earnings may be impacted by a potential electricity tariff cut in Thailand, CGS International's Amornrat Cheevavichawalkul says in a research report, as the brokerage lowers its target price to THB32.50 from THB43.50. The Energy Regulatory Commission of Thailand has proposed a reduction in electricity tariff to THB3.98/unit, versus the current THB4.15/unit, by removing the adder tariff and feed-in-tariff from the electricity cost structure, the analyst notes. The brokerage estimates that every THB1/unit reduction in the electricity tariff should lead to a 12.8% decline in the Thai power company's 2025 EPS. The brokerage maintains its hold rating as concerns over potentially lower tariffs should mostly be priced in. Shares are 5.9% lower at THB28.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - Samsung Heavy Industries is expected to exceed its 2025 guidance for new contract wins, Nomura analyst Eon Hwang writes in a note. Hwang expects new orders for the South Korean shipbuilder to jump 59% to $12 billion this year, beating its own target of $9.8 billion. Samsung Heavy's operating profit is projected to rise 54% in 2025, Hwang says. He is also upbeat on Samsung Heavy bidding for two floating liquefied natural gas projects this year--one off Mozambique and the other in the U.S. Nomura raises its target price for the stock by 14% to KRW16,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.8% lower at KRW13,160. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0506 GMT - The magnitude of price cuts in China's electric-vehicle industry this year should be much less than last year, Jefferies analysts write in a note. Since inventories at BYD dealers are much lower and the profitability of new car sales is much better, the analysts reckon BYD is unlikely to kick off an aggressive price war like last year. Among the top Chinese EV makers, Xiaomi, Huawei-backed Harmony Intelligent Mobility Alliance and XPeng are outperformers by new order intakes, they note. The introduction of Tesla's full-self driving features in China this year could serve as a good example for its Chinese rivals on how carmakers can commercialize their advanced assisted driving technologies, they add. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0505 GMT - The suspension of a U.S. trade provision allowing goods valued under $800 to enter the country duty-free will likely have an immediate negative impact on Chinese exporters, Nomura analysts say in a research note. Without the de minimis exemption, direct-to-consumer exporters would need to obtain approvals from various regulatory agencies and be subject to higher tariffs, the analysts say. Nomura estimates that 10% of Chinese shipments would no longer be able to enter the U.S. as a result. The analysts expect consumer demand to roughly halve, correlating with an expected 55% price hike. Meanwhile, they estimate that Chinese goods entering the U.S. under the de minimis exemption could plummet 60%. Therefore, ending the de minimis provision could shave off 1.3 percentage points from China's export growth and 0.2 percentage point from GDP growth. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Accumulate GE Vernova T&D India; target of Rs 1950: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on GE Vernova T&D India

GE Vernova T&D India (GVTD) delivered a strong quarterly performance, registering a 28.0% YoY revenue growth alongside a notable EBITDA margin expansion of 523bps YoY to 16.7%. The company's growth trajectory is underpinned by a robust domestic order pipeline, driven by increasing investments in India’s power sector, a growing focus on grid efficiency, and a healthy pipeline of HVDC projects. As power grids become more interconnected and reliant on digital technologies, the need for enhanced grid security has intensified, bolstering demand for GVTD’s grid integration and software solutions. Management remains confident that the strong order intake momentum will sustain through Q4, further strengthening its revenue visibility. Additionally, the company's cost-competitive manufacturing capabilities position it as a key supplier for the global GE Vernova network, driving export demand. With disciplined cost-control initiatives, GVTD aims to sustain EBITDA margins in the mid-to-high teens, despite ongoing supply chain challenges.

Outlook

We believe 1) a healthy order pipeline in the power market, 2) a robust order book (Rs107.8bn), and 3) the management’s focus on margin improvement augur well for strong revenue & profit growth. The stock is trading at a P/E of 55.5x/42.7 on FY26/27E. We maintain ‘Accumulate’ rating with a TP of Rs1,950 (Rs1,946 earlier) valuing the stock at a PE of 55x Sep’26E (same as earlier).

data.title: Pernod Ricard Lowers Guidance on Economic, Geopolitical Uncertainties
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

French liquor group Pernod Ricard lowered its expectations for its fiscal year, citing economic and geopolitical uncertainties that continue to hit the spirits industry.

The maker of Absolut Vodka anticipates a low single-digit decline in organic net sales for the year ending June and its aiming to sustain its organic operating margin. It had previously forecast a return to sales growth for fiscal 2025.

Subject to the challenges posed by the global trade environment and prospect of new tariffs, the group expects fiscal 2026 to be a transitional year, with improving trends in organic net sales.

"Amid extraordinary trade tensions, we are focused on defending organic operating margin to the fullest extent possible," it said.

Pernod Ricard reported sales of 6.18 billion euros ($6.43 billion) for the first half of its fiscal year, with an organic decline of 4% on year. In reported terms, sales were 6% lower than the prior-year period.

The group booked net profit of 1.19 billion euros, down 24% on year.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Warpaint London Expects FY24 Pretax Profit, Revenue to Climb
data.description:
Warpaint London estimates its profit before tax and revenue for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, to increase year over year.

The British color cosmetics company said Thursday it projects revenue to be 102 million pounds sterling, compared with 89.6 million pounds a year ago. It also expects pretax profit to rise to 24 million pounds from 18.1 million pounds.

data.title: Max Financial Services Target Price Raised to INR1,300 From INR1,250 by Nomura >500271.BY
Max Financial Services Raised to Buy From Neutral by Nomura >500271.BY
Anglo American's FY24 Diamond, Copper, PGM Output Falls; Iron Ore Nickel Production Up
data.description:
Anglo American (, AGL.JO) said Thursday that production declined year over year for all its products except for iron ore in full-year 2024.

In the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, output decreased to 773,000 tonnes from 826,000 tonnes for copper; 3.6 million ounces from 3.8 million ounces for platinum group metals; 24.7 million karats from 31.9 million karats for diamonds; 14.5 million tonnes from 16 million tonnes for steelmaking coal; 39,400 tonnes from 40,000 tonnes for nickel; and 2.3 million tonnes from 3.7 million tonnes for manganese ore.

On the other hand, iron ore production increased year over year to 60.8 million tonnes from 59.9 million tonnes.

Looking ahead to full-year 2025, the company expects copper production between 690,000 tonnes and 750,000 tonnes; iron ore production between 57 million tonnes and 61 million tonnes, PGM production between 3 million ounces and 3.4 million ounces, diamonds production between 20 million carats and 23 million carats, steelmaking coal between 10 million tonnes and 12 million tonnes and nickel production between 37,000 tonnes and 39,000 tonnes.

data.title: A.P Moller-Maersk Swings to Profit as Red Sea Shipping Disruptions Boost Rates
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

A.P. Moeller-Maersk swung to a net profit in the fourth quarter that exceeded analysts' expectations, as disruptions to the Red Sea shipping supported higher shipping rates.

The Danish shipping company said it made $2.085 billion in net profit for the quarter, compared with a net loss of $436 million a year ago, when an oversupply of cargo capacity weighed on freight rates.

Quarterly revenue came to $14.59 billion, a 24% on-year increase as continued disruption to shipping lanes in the Red Sea pushed up shipping rates by 38% in the quarter.

The result compares with analysts' expectations of $1.31 billion in net profit on $13.89 billion in revenue, according to a Visible Alpha consensus.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: RAK Cement to Halt Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
Ras Al Khaimah Co. for White Cement & Construction Materials (ADX:RAKWCT), d/b/a RAK Cement, shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 9 am local time on Feb. 13 due to a board meeting.

The cement manufacturer will resume trading, pending the publication of its meeting results, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Kering's Gucci Parts Ways With Creative Director Sabato De Sarno
data.description:
(07:30 GMT) *Kering: New Gucci Artistic Direction Will Be Announced in Due Time
(07:45 GMT) Kering's Gucci Parts Ways With Creative Director Sabato De Sarno

By Andrea Figueras

Kering's flagship label Gucci said it ended its collaboration with creative director Sabato De Sarno, at a time when the brand is facing weak demand and as the French group seeks to rejuvenate its fashion houses.

The fall-winter 2025 fashion show in Milan, which will take place on Feb. 25, will be presented by the Gucci design office, the company said Thursday. A new artistic direction will be announced in due time.

The move follows a number of changes at the French luxury giant led by Francois-Henri Pinault. In December, the group appointed a new creative director for its Bottega Veneta brand. It had previously named new chief executives at Saint Laurent and Balenciaga.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: China Shares Gain Following USPS Package Ban Reversal; Bank of Ningbo Down More than 1%
data.description:
Chinese shares logged strong gains on Thursday after the US Postal Service reversed its ban on mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.

The Shanghai Composite Index, the main gauge of Chinese stocks, rose 1.3% to 3,270.66. The Shenzhen Component Index jumped 2.3% to 10,393.63.

The USPS will now continue accepting international inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong, reversing a previously announced suspension. It said it will now work with the Customs and Border Protection to implement an efficient collection mechanism for the new tariffs to curb disruptions in package deliveries.

Meanwhile, China's foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian called for a dialogue "based on equality and mutual respect" between China and the US after US tariffs on all Chinese goods kicked in. With the European Union also facing potential US tariffs, Lin pushed for deeper cooperation with the bloc to tackle global challenges.

In company news, Bank of Ningbo posted net profit attributable to shareholders of 27.1 billion yuan in 2024, up 6.2% from 25.5 billion yuan the previous year. Shares of the bank closed more than 1% lower Thursday.

data.title: Buy Sunteck Realty; target of Rs 700: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Sunteck Realty

We recently attended SRIN’s analyst day and interacted with the senior management. The company’s proven ability to market ultra-luxury projects, and aggressive and multi-pronged land acquisition capabilities in various micro markets across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) are an interesting play on Mumbai’s high-value real estate market. We expect the company’s pre-sales to grow to +30% CAGR over FY24-27E, led by ongoing projects and a strong new launch pipeline, including the Dubai JV project. Further, given the likely strong cash flow generation and JV with IFC, SRIN is likely to step up new project additions, which will act as a catalyst for stock performance.

Outlook

Adoption of an asset light model has enabled the company to acquire scale without straining its balance sheet. We maintain ‘BUY’ rating with SOTP-based TP of Rs700/share.

data.title: Accumulate GAIL India; target of Rs 204: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on GAIL India

GAIL’s integrated pipeline tariff was set at Rs58.6/mmBtu in Apr’2023. The company submitted a tariff petition in Aug’24 citing rise in domestic gas prices over recent years as well as volume considerations. GAIL has proposed a tariff rise from Rs58.6/mmBtu to Rs78/mmBtu. However, past few tariff orders for GAIL and GSPL suggest that PNGRB has often set tariffs lower than what companies propose. According to our analysis, increase in gas price for SUG would lead to a Rs4-5/mmBtu increase in its tariff to ~Rs63/mmBtu.

Outlook

Given this potential upward revision in its tariff and normalization of trading margins in Q4, we reiterate ‘Accumulate’ rating on the stock with a TP of Rs204 based on 12x FY27 adj EPS and adding the value of investments.

(07:14 GMT) *Carlsberg 2024 Net Pft DKK9.12B
(07:14 GMT) *Analysts Saw Carlsberg 2024 Net Pft at DKK6.78B
(07:36 GMT) Carlsberg Beats Profit Expectations, Warns of Slower Growth Ahead

By Michael Susin

Carlsberg beat profit expectations in 2024 but said it expects slower growth due to the loss of the San Miguel brand license in the U.K.

The Danish brewer, maker of the namesake lager brand as well as other beers and ciders, expects organic growth in operating profit of between 1% and 5% in 2025. The target includes an anticipated negative impact of two to three percentage points from the loss of the San Miguel brand in the U.K.

For 2024, the company swung to a net profit of 9.12 billion Danish kronor ($1.27 billion), ahead of the company-compiled market views of 6.78 billion kronor. A year earlier, it had booked a loss of 40.79 billion kronor after a significant write-off related to the sale of its Russian Baltika breweries business, which was seized by the authorities.

Adjusted operating profit--the company's preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--rose to 11.41 billion kronor from 11.11 billion kronor. However, it was below market expectations of 11.47 billion kronor. The miss was driven by foreign exchange volatility, including the impact of hyperinflation in Laos.

Organic operating profit growth came in at 6%, at the top line of the company's guidance.

Revenue rose 1.9% on a reported basis to 75.01 billion kronor. This was ahead of analysts' expectations of 74.965 billion kronor, according to consensus estimates compiled by the company.

Carlsberg declared a final dividend of 27 kroner a share, unchanged from a year ago.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Accumulate Zydus Lifesciences; target of Rs 1050: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Zydus Lifesciences

Zydus Lifesciences (ZYDUSLIF) Q3 adjusted EBITDA for one offs was below our estimate led by lower GMs and higher R&D spend. We believe the company’s steady domestic franchise, strong balance sheet and potential new launches in the US will help negate pricing pressure and likely competition in some key products like gAsacol. Moreover, ZYDUSLIF is working on a robust pipeline of complex products, including injectables, 505(b)2, transdermals, NCE, biosimilars and vaccines, which are expected to materialize over the next 2–3 years. Mgmt have guided for 2-3 high value launches over F27/28, which will keep momentum in US sales healthy.

Outlook

Our FY26/27E EPS stands broadly remains unchanged. We maintain our ‘Accumulate’ rating with TP of Rs1,050, valuing at 25x FY27E EPS.

data.title: Enlight Media's Box Office Revenue Reaches 4.84 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Beijing Enlight Media generated a box office revenue amounting to 4.84 billion yuan after releasing the movie Nezha: The Devil Boy Conquers the Dragon King in China, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

The movie exceeded 50% of the company's audited consolidated financial statements for the most recent fiscal year.

The Chinese film production company's operating income as of Tuesday, Feb. 4, is expected to be between 950 million yuan and 1.01 billion yuan.

The company's shares soared 18% at the close.

data.title: Reduce Bajaj Electricals; target of Rs 647: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Bajaj Electricals

We downward revise Bajaj Electricals (BJE) FY25/26/27E earnings by 6.8%/4.3%/4.3% factoring soft revenue growth in CP segment and 7% EBITDA margin guidance. CP segment grew 7.5% YoY on account of festive demand (accounted for 81% revenue), which led to strong double-digit growth in Domestic Appliances and Morphy Richards, while Fans & Kitchen Appliances reported flat growth in Q3FY25. Lighting revenue degrew 7.5% due to continuous price erosion. Lighting EBIT declined due to investment in brandbuilding activities and one-time impact of warranty provision made in Q3FY24. BJE aims to achieve 7% EBITDA margin by FY27 with reduction in logistics costs and improvement in operating leverage. The company indicated that trade is showing signs of recovery with double-digit growth, while we expect the CP segment to log 8.6% CAGR over FY24-27E.

Outlook

We estimate FY24-27E revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of 8.3%/16.5%/17.4%. We value the stock at 35x FY27 EPS and arrive at TP of Rs647 (earlier Rs676). Maintain ‘REDUCE’ rating.

data.title: BOE To Cut Rates for 3rd Time
data.description:
The Bank of England is expected to lower its benchmark bank rate by 25bps to 4.5% at its February 2025 meeting, the lowest level since early 2023, marking the third cut since it began reducing borrowing costs in August last year.

Traders will closely watch for signals on the BoE’s policy outlook for the rest of the year, though a cautious approach to easing is anticipated.

Markets have priced in three rate cuts for 2025.

Additionally, the BoE's quarterly Monetary Policy Report is expected to reflect downgraded growth forecasts and higher inflation projections for the year.

UK inflation slowed to 2.5% in December, while economic growth remains weak, with GDP rising just 0.1% in November after a 0.1% decline in October, following stagnation in Q3.

Meanwhile, the labor market is showing signs of cooling, with the unemployment rate inching up to 4.4% in the three months to November.

Finally, traders will look for any BoE commentary on the recent government budget and US tariffs.

data.title: Monte dei Paschi Hikes Dividend Despite Profit Fall
data.description:
(07:09 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi Year-End Fully Loaded CET1 Ratio 18.2%
(07:13 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi 4Q Rev EUR996.4M
(07:14 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi 4Q Net Pft EUR384.9M
(07:14 GMT) *Analysts Saw Monte dei Paschi 4Q Revenue at EUR973.8M
(07:14 GMT) *Analysts Saw Monte dei Paschi 4Q Net Profit at EUR294.5M
(07:14 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi 4Q Net Interest Income EUR588M
(07:14 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi 4Q Net Fee and Commission Income EUR373.5M
(07:16 GMT) *Monte dei Paschi Raises Dividend to EUR0.86 Vs EUR0.25 a Share
(07:38 GMT) Monte dei Paschi Hikes Dividend Despite Profit Fall

By Adria Calatayud

Monte dei Paschi raised its dividend payout and reported a fourth-quarter net profit that declined but exceeded analysts' expectations.

The Italian bank, formally called Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, last month launched a near-$14 billion offer to buy counterpart Mediobanca, joining the rush to consolidate Europe's banking system. Mediobanca's board rejected the bid, saying it would destroy value for shareholders of both banks.

Monte dei Paschi, considered the world's oldest bank still in operation, said it plans to pay out more than 1 billion euros ($1.04 billion) in dividends to shareholders. It proposed a dividend of 0.86 euro a share, up from 0.25 euros a share a year ago.

Net profit for the quarter was 384.9 million euros, down from 1.12 billion euros in the year-earlier period, when the bank's result was boosted by releases of provisions for litigation risks.

Revenue grew to 996.4 million euros from 992.5 million euros, after higher net fee and commission income offset a decline in net interest income--the difference between what banks earns on loans and pay on deposits.

Analysts had forecast a net profit of 294.5 million euros and revenue of 973.8 million euros, according to consensus estimates provided by Visible Alpha.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: CRH Selling Stake in U.K.'s SigmaRoc for Around $143.8 Million
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

CRH said it is selling its shares in U.K. building-material company SigmaRoc for around 115 million pounds, the equivalent of $143.8 million.

The Irish building-materials supplier said Thursday that, subject to completion, it would sell 171.6 million ordinary shares at 67 pence a share for a total of about 115 million pounds.

On Wednesday, the company said it would sell up to 172 million shares via a bookbuild, and that this represented around 15.4% of Sigmaroc's issued share capital.

The company said that following completion it will have exited its interest in SigmaRoc in full.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Trent Logs Increase in Fiscal Q3 Consolidated Net Profit
data.description:
Trent recorded a rise in its consolidated attributable net profit to 4.97 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter from 3.74 billion rupees a year ago, according to a Thursday filing by the retailer.

Earnings per share during the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, climbed to 13.99 rupees from 10.53 rupees a year earlier.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 also increased to 46.6 billion rupees from 34.7 billion rupees a year ago.

data.title: Adnoc Gas Net Profit Beats Market Expectations
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Adnoc Gas, one of the world's biggest natural-gas producers, reported fourth-quarter net income that beat market expectations, and said it expects capital expenditure to rise this year.

The Abu Dhabi-owned gas company on Thursday said net profit for the quarter rose to $1.38 billion from $1.35 billion it reported for the same period a year earlier, beating the $1.03 billion analysts polled by Visible Alpha had expected.

The company said lower volumes hurt its result, although this was partially offset by better domestic margins.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose 3% on year to $2.28 billion.

Fourth-quarter sales volumes were 869 trillion British thermal units, down 5% on the same period a year earlier, hit by planned shutdowns.

For 2025, Adnoc said it expects capital expenditure to rise to between $3.0 billion and $3.5 billion from $1.84 billion last year due to higher spending on its ethane recovery and monetization project and maintenance across its business.

It expects domestic gas production for the year of between 2,320 trillion and 2,390 trillion British thermal units compared with the 2,330 trillion BTU it produced in 2024.

For the first quarter, Adnoc said it expects a decline in net income from the previous quarter due to production shutdowns at liquefied natural gas and other sites which will have an impact of at least $100 million.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com
